name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ لَوْلَا يُكَلِّمُنَا ٱللَّهُ أَوْ تَأْتِينَآ ءَايَةٌ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّثْلَ قَوْلِهِمْ ۘ تَشَـٰبَهَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ قَدْ بَيَّنَّا ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (١١٨)
```

```english
Those who have no knowledge say, ‘Why does not Allah speak to us, or come to us a sign?’ So said those who were before them, [words] similar to what they say. Alike are their hearts. We have certainly made the signs clear for a people who have certainty. (118)
```

```arabic
إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا ۖ وَلَا تُسْـَٔلُ عَنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (١١٩)
```

```english
Indeed We have sent you with the truth, as a bearer of good news and as a warner, and you will not be questioned concerning the inmates of hell. (119)
```

## (Commentary)

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وقال الذين لا يعلمون** — **And those who do not know say...** (2:118). These are the polytheists (المشركون) other than the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب). This is indicated by the preceding contrast in His saying, the Exalted: **وقالت اليهود ليست النصارى على شيء وقالت النصارى ليست اليهود على شيء وهم يتلون الكتاب كذلك قال الذين لا يعلمون مثل قولهم** — **The Jews say, ‘The Christians stand on nothing,’ and the Christians say, ‘The Jews stand on nothing,’ though they recite the Book. Even so do those who do not know say the like of their saying** (2:113). In that verse, the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) were joined in their speech to the polytheists and the faithless (الكفار) among the Arabs; whereas in this verse, the polytheists and the faithless are joined to them. Thus He said: **وقال الذين لا يعلمون لولا يكلمنا الله أو تأتينا آية** — **And those who do not know say, ‘Why does not Allah speak to us, or a sign come to us?’** (2:118). **كذلك قال الذين من قبلهم** — **Even so said those who were before them** (2:118)—and these are the People of the Book, specifically the Jews among them—when they proposed similar sayings to the Prophet of Allah, Moses (عليه‌السلام). Thus, they and the faithless are alike in their thoughts and opinions; these say what those said, and vice versa: **تشابهت قلوبهم** — **Their hearts are alike** (2:118).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **قد بينا الآيات لقوم يوقنون** — **We have certainly made the signs clear for a people who have certainty** (2:118). This is a response to the saying of "those who do not know," etc. The meaning is that the signs (الآيات) they demand have already been brought and made clear, but they do not benefit anyone except a people who possess certainty (يوقنون) regarding the signs of Allah. As for these who "do not know," their hearts are veiled by the shroud of ignorance (الجهل) and afflicted by the maladies of prejudice (العصبية) and stubbornness (العناد); and signs avail nothing for a people who do not know. From this, the reason for describing them as lacking knowledge (عدم العلم) becomes apparent. He then reinforced this by directing the discourse to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), indicating that he is sent from Allah with the truth as a bearer of good news (بشيراً) and a warner (نذيراً). Therefore, let his soul be at peace, and let him know that these are the inmates of hell (أصحاب الجحيم), for whom this fate is decreed; there is no hope for their guidance or salvation.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ولا تسأل عن أصحاب الجحيم** — **and you will not be questioned about the inmates of hell** (2:119). This follows the same course as His saying: **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَأَنذَرْتَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تُنذِرْهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ** — **As for the faithless, it is the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will not have faith** (2:6).
